management_notes:
Executive Chairman Ari Sussman has one of the most relevant track records in junior mining: he founded Continental Gold Inc. in 2010, built the Buriticá gold deposit in Colombia from grassroots to an 11+ Moz high-grade discovery, and sold the company to Zijin Mining for approximately C$2 billion in 2020. This is precisely the playbook being executed at Collective Mining's Guayabales Project. The Colombia expertise, management relationships in-country, and understanding of how to build and sell a high-grade Colombian gold asset are directly transferable. President Omar Ossma and EVP Exploration Russell Evans (appointed March 2026, formerly with Newmont and Vedanta/Hindustan Zinc) round out the executive team.

Agnico Eagle Mines holds approximately 14.99% of Collective Mining's shares with board nomination rights and investor participation rights in future financings — the most strategically significant minority investment available in the junior mining market. Agnico has deployed C$63.4M in March 2025 (at C$11.00/share), exercised 2.25M warrants, and then participated in the October 2025 C$140M bought deal at C$19.00/share. Three tranches of strategic investment from Agnico Eagle at increasing prices is among the strongest institutional endorsements in the market.

A Morpheus Research short report (August 2025) alleged drilling on untitled land and within environmentally protected riparian buffer zones, triggering securities class action investigations. The company categorically rejected the allegations. This short thesis remains an unresolved overhang that represents regulatory and reputational risk that should be independently verified before any significant capital allocation.
geology_notes:
The Guayabales Project (Caldas Department, Middle Cauca Belt, Colombia) hosts the Apollo intrusion-related gold-silver-tungsten-copper system, which has been drilled to over 1,300 metres vertical depth across 167,500+ total metres and has not been closed at depth or along strike. Key intercepts include: 486.00 m at 2.01 g/t AuEq (APC-141), 467.35 m at 1.63 g/t AuEq, 165.75 m at 5.35 g/t AuEq including 31.70 m at 17.35 g/t AuEq, and 111.15 m at 5.48 g/t AuEq from surface. These are genuinely exceptional drill widths and grades for an intrusion-related system.

The San Antonio Project (discovered May 2025) adds a gold-copper porphyry discovery 290.20 m at 0.84 g/t AuEq (discovery hole), expanded to 172.40 m at 1.88 g/t AuEq, with a high-grade silver-dominant vein system outlined over 825 m strike (January 2026). The discovery of a porphyry system adjacent to the Apollo epithermal system significantly increases the district-scale geological optionality of the Guayabales land package. Management targets a maiden NI 43-101 resource at Apollo exceeding 10 Moz AuEq, with the maiden resource estimate now targeted for H1 2027 (delayed from Q4 2026).

No published NI 43-101 resource estimate exists — this is the fundamental limitation of the geological score. All valuation is based on drill intercepts and management's resource target. The quality of the intercepts (thickness, grade, depth extension) is consistent with a major system, but without a resource, the geological score cannot exceed 4 regardless of intercept quality. The Morpheus drilling-on-untitled-land allegation, if true, would have regulatory implications for resource certification.
capital_notes:
Collective Mining has approximately 92.3 million basic shares outstanding — a tight share structure by TSX standards. Cash is approximately US$135M (~C$187M) as of December 2025, following the October 2025 C$140M bought deal at C$19.00/share (with Agnico participating for C$15M of the deal). The company is included in the GDXJ index and MSCI Canada Small Cap Index. The C$2.0B market cap at approximately C$21.50/share is a large valuation for a pre-resource company, implying significant market conviction in resource delivery.

The capital structure is clean: 92.3M basic shares, US$135M cash, no debt. The cash position provides at least 2 years of runway for the up-to-100,000-metre 2026 drill program across Guayabales and San Antonio, plus the geological work required to support the maiden resource estimate in H1 2027. No financings are expected to be required before the maiden resource announcement, which eliminates a near-term dilution risk.

The primary capital risk is the burn rate: running 9+ drill rigs in Colombia simultaneously is capital-intensive, and the 2026 budget has not been fully detailed in public disclosures. If the maiden resource disappoints in size or grade, the C$2.0B market cap would require a sharp downward re-rating, and the subsequent ability to raise capital at current prices would be impaired.
catalyst_notes:
The Apollo maiden NI 43-101 resource estimate is the defining catalyst for Collective Mining, now targeted for H1 2027 (delayed from the prior Q4 2026 target). At a C$2.0B market cap with no published resource, the stock is trading on the expectation of resource delivery — the delay from Q4 2026 to H1 2027 is a minor setback that reflects the complexity of the system and the time required to complete the resource database. Continuous drill results from the up-to-100,000-metre 2026 program across Apollo and San Antonio are expected every 4-8 weeks, maintaining news flow.

The San Antonio porphyry expansion (high-grade silver vein system over 825 m strike, January 2026) adds a separate active geological catalyst. EVP Exploration Russell Evans' appointment in March 2026 (with Newmont and Vedanta pedigree) is a strategic hire suggesting the company is transitioning from discovery-drilling to resource-delineation discipline.

The Morpheus Research short thesis (August 2025) is an ongoing overhang — any regulatory action from Colombian mining authorities (ANM) relating to the alleged title or environmental issues would be a major negative catalyst. Management's categorical rejection should be independently verified before the resource estimate process can be considered fully unimpaired.
acquisition_notes:
At a C$2.0B enterprise value with no published resource, Collective Mining is pricing in meaningful resource delivery. Management's 10+ Moz AuEq target at Apollo would imply an EV/oz of approximately US$95-100/oz at current prices — modest for a high-grade system in a Latin American jurisdiction, particularly if the deposit is comparable to Continental Gold's Buriticá (which sold for ~US$130/oz on reserves). The pre-resource valuation makes a direct EV/oz comparison premature, but the Apollo scale of intercepts is consistent with a major deposit.

Agnico Eagle's escalating investment pattern — initial entry (February 2024), then C$63.4M at C$11.00 (March 2025), then participation in the C$140M deal at C$19.00 (October 2025) — is the textbook pattern of a strategic acquirer building a blocking position. Agnico's investor rights agreement provides participation rights in future financings, effectively ensuring they can maintain or increase their 14.99% stake through to any eventual transaction. The Continental Gold/Zijin precedent (C$2B for 11 Moz at Buriticá) is the reference transaction for what a completed Columbia high-grade gold acquisition looks like.

The Morpheus short report's allegations, if substantiated by Colombian mining authorities, would have material implications for resource certification and acquisition readiness. This regulatory risk must be monitored and is the primary factor preventing a higher acquisition score.
analyst_summary:
BUY (20/25). Collective Mining's investment thesis is grounded in two powerful signals: Ari Sussman's proven track record of building and selling a C$2B Colombian high-grade gold deposit, and Agnico Eagle's three-tranche escalating investment to 14.99% with board rights and participation rights in future financing. The Apollo system's drill intercepts — including 486 m at 2.01 g/t AuEq and multiple thick, high-grade intervals — are among the most impressive pre-resource drill results in the global junior gold pipeline. San Antonio's porphyry discovery adds district-scale geological upside beyond Apollo.

The primary risks are clear: no published NI 43-101 resource (H1 2027 target is 12+ months away), a C$2.0B pre-resource market cap that prices in full resource delivery, and the unresolved Morpheus Research short thesis alleging regulatory violations. The high cash burn from 9+ drill rigs and the delayed resource timeline add execution uncertainty. Colombia political/regulatory risk is a persistent background factor that cannot be fully modelled.

The key catalyst to watch is the Apollo maiden NI 43-101 resource estimate (H1 2027). A resource of 10+ Moz AuEq at grades comparable to the drill intercepts would validate the market valuation and likely catalyse a formal acquisition process from Agnico Eagle or a competing bidder. Monitor any Colombian mining authority (ANM) communications regarding title verification and buffer zone compliance — resolution of the Morpheus allegations would remove a significant risk factor before the resource release.
